sections.title: Pre-contract and contractual disclosures for consumer products
sections.summary: Institutions must disclose all terms and conditions, features, risks, benefits, fees, and charges to consumers before contract execution. They must provide a Key Facts Statement (KFS) for loans, allow a 2-day review period, and grant a 3-day cooling-off period after signing. Contracts must include interest rates, fees, and mechanisms for variation notifications. Institutions must notify consumers of transaction activity and provide monthly statements at no cost.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires detailed pre-contract and ongoing disclosures for financial products, impacting data sharing transparency and consumer consent mechanisms in cross-border transactions involving Nigerian financial institutions.

requirements.text: Disclose all terms and conditions of a product or service on offer, as well as features, risks, benefits, fees and associated charges
Disclose possibility of interest rate or foreign exchange variations due to market conditions
Provide Key Facts Statement (KFS) in Annexure A format for consumer loans
Allow consumers minimum of two (2) days from receipt to review draft contract before execution
Grant three (3) working day cooling-off period after signing credit contract for cancellation without penalty
Notify consumers of transactions immediately with date, amount, payer, transaction reference, channel and cleared balance
Ensure account statements include transaction reference, payer, transaction channel, location and account balance
Provide monthly statements for all products and account types at no cost
Give prior notice of interest rate variations with reason, commencement date, revised repayment schedule and alternatives
Ensure variations in interest rates implemented after 10 working days (for DMBs) or 5 working days (for other institutions)

sections.source_quote: Institutions shall comply with the following requirements prior to the execution of any contract with consumers: Disclose all terms and conditions of a product or service on offer, as well as the features, risks, benefits, fees and other associated charges.

sections.title: Responsible business conduct and consumer protection
sections.summary: Institutions must conduct business professionally and ethically, train staff, provide clear product information, and establish complaint channels. They must not impose products on consumers, charge for unused services, or require third-party products as conditions for credit. Credit assessments must consider financial capability and include credit history checks.
sections.dtia_summary: Establishes ethical business practices that impact data sharing transparency and consumer consent mechanisms in cross-border financial transactions involving Nigerian institutions.

requirements.text: Conduct business in responsible, professional and ethical manner
Train staff to promote competence, efficiency and professionalism
Provide clear information about products, services, features, terms and conditions
Provide channels for consumer enquiries and complaints
Notify customers of circumstances affecting contract terms
Display all applicable fees for electronic payments with option to accept or decline
Not impose any product, service or channel on consumers
Not charge fees for issuance and renewal of products/services not requested by customer
Not compel consumers to buy products/services as condition for another product/service unless directly dependent
Assess consumer repayment capability before granting credit
Ascertain credit history from Credit Risk Management System, Credit Bureau and other sources
Have policy to deal with consumers in financial difficulty

sections.source_quote: Institutions shall comply with the following provisions: Conduct their business in a responsible, professional and ethical manner.

sections.title: Complaints handling and redress mechanisms
sections.summary: Institutions must establish complaint channels, internal dispute resolution processes, and external escalation to the Central Bank of Nigeria. They must respond to consumer inquiries within five working days and provide timely resolution for complaints without undue delay.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires institutions to implement robust complaints handling mechanisms that impact data sharing transparency and consumer rights in cross-border financial transactions.

requirements.text: Establish complaint management channels for consumers
Implement internal dispute resolution processes
Provide external dispute resolution at Central Bank of Nigeria
Respond to customer enquiries within five (5) working days

sections.source_quote: Institutions shall comply with the following requirements: Establish complaint management channels, internal dispute resolution and external escalation to the Central Bank of Nigeria.

sections.heading: (continued)
sections.title: Consumer loan guarantees and debt collection requirements
sections.summary: Institutions must ensure loan guarantees are properly executed with independent advice or declaration of no need for advice. They must provide periodic loan performance updates to guarantors, issue discharge letters within 5 business days post-loan liquidation, and notify customers of declined loan requests with reasons. Guarantors must be informed of loan liquidation. Institutions must also ensure debt collection processes are transparent, courteous, and fair without undue pressure or harassment.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires institutions to maintain clear communication with guarantors and customers regarding loan status and discharges, with specific timelines for discharge letters and notifications. These conditions directly impact cross-border data transfers involving loan guarantees and debt collection.

requirements.text: Sought and obtained independent professional advice before executing the guarantee
Declared that they do not need independent advice
Provide periodic update to guarantors on loan performance
Issue letter of discharge at no cost to customers within 5 business days after liquidation
Notify customers where loan requests are declined with reasons upon request
Engage and give customers notice of outstanding obligations prior to debt collection
Initiate foreclosures only after other recovery steps fail
Give customers option for private sale within 30 days before foreclosure
Apply net proceeds from foreclosures to loan accounts and inform customers of balance
Provide report of collateral sale including process, proceeds, expenses, and net proceeds
Be liable for actions of debt collection agents
Not contact friends, employers, relatives or neighbors without consent or for employment status
Not require persons to offset debt without being guarantor
Not make telephone contact with customers between 9pm and 8am without consent

sections.source_quote: 5.2.8 Provide periodic update to Guarantors on the loan performance to enable them to assess the likelihood of being called upon to discharge their commitments. 5.2.9 Issue Letter of Discharge at no cost to customers within 5 business days after liquidation of their loans, and where the loan is guaranteed, the guarantor shall also be notified accordingly. 5.2.10 Notify customers where their loan requests are declined. The reason(s) for decline shall be provided upon request.

sections.heading: (continued)
sections.title: Complaints handling and dispute resolution procedures
sections.summary: Institutions must develop documented complaint handling processes with fairness, transparency, and responsiveness. They must allocate resources for skilled staff, disclose complaint channels and timelines at all customer engagement points, and provide multiple complaint channels including verbal complaints. Institutions must also establish internal dispute resolution (IDR) mechanisms with 24-hour acknowledgment, tracking numbers, and resolution within specified timelines. They must issue summary resolution communications and handle refunds for excess charges.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires institutions to implement robust complaint handling systems with clear timelines and communication protocols, which directly impact data transfer and processing activities involving customer complaints and dispute resolution.

requirements.text: Develop documented processes for complaints handling ensuring fairness, transparency, responsiveness and independence
Allocate adequate resources for complaints handling with skilled and well-trained staff
Disclose complaint channels, contact details, resolution timelines and escalation options at all customer engagement points
Provide multiple dedicated channels for receiving and handling complaints including verbal complaints
Be effective, functional, efficient and accessible always for complaint channels
Generate unique identification number and acknowledge complaints within 24 hours of lodgment
Accord special consideration to needs of vulnerable groups including physically challenged persons and seniors
Provide emergency channels for time-sensitive issues especially fraud-related complaints
Establish internal dispute resolution (IDR) structures and processes for consumer complaints
Communicate acknowledgment within 24 hours including unique tracking number, contact details, expected timeline, escalation options and assurance of address
Use unique tracking number in all correspondence with complainant
Designate complaints handling unit independent of other business functions
Require staff handling complaints to declare conflicts of interest
Resolve complaints within timelines specified in Annexure D
Refund identified excess charges, non-payment or under-payment of interest, unauthorized debits with interest as per CBN guidelines
Issue letter of apology where refunds are made
Reconstruct customers' accounts to identify excess charges and pay interest after reconstruction
Remit established refunds and interest to complainants' accounts within prescribed timelines
Send summary resolution communication (SRC) within next business day with basis for decision, right of appeal and escalation options
Consider complaint resolved after 30 days of written acceptance of resolution
Provide 2nd level review within timelines for complaints after 30 days of SRC receipt
Identify recurring complaint types, root causes, implement remedial measures and generate Root Cause Analysis Report (RCAR)
Prepare quarterly RCAR and submit to CBN by 14th day of January, April, July, October

sections.source_quote: 6.1.1 Develop documented processes on Complaints Handling that will guarantee fairness, transparency, responsiveness and independence of the complaint handling mechanism. 6.1.2 Allocate adequate resources for complaints handling/redress and the processes manned by skilled and well-trained members of staff. 6.1.3 At all customer engagement points, disclose channels and contact details for lodging complaints, timeframe for resolution, and options for escalation.

sections.heading: below:
sections.title: Penalties for non-compliance with consumer protection regulations
sections.summary: Institutions face specific penalties for violations including non-resolution of complaints within timelines (N500,000 per complaint per week), non-acknowledgment of complaints (N2,000,00:00 per complaint), non-response to CBN directives (N2,000,000), false reporting (N100,000 plus N10,000 per day), and administrative sanctions for persistent breaches.
sections.dtia_summary: Penalties for non-compliance with complaint handling procedures directly impact financial institutions' data transfer and processing activities, particularly in cross-border contexts where complaint resolution timelines and data sharing practices are critical.

requirements.text: Penalty of N500,000 per complaint per week for non-resolution within prescribed timelines
Penalty of N2,000,000 per complaint for non-acknowledgment of complaints
Penalty of N2,000,000 for non-response to CBN directives
Penalty of N100,000 plus N10,000 per day for false or non-rendition of returns/reports
Administrative sanctions for persistent breaches of consumer protection regulations

sections.source_quote: 7. Non-resolution of complaints within timelines: N500,000 per complaint per week. Non-acknowledgment of complaints: N2,000,000 per complaint. Non-response to CBN directives: N2,000,000. False reporting: N100,000 plus N10,000 per day. Administrative sanctions for persistent breaches.

sections.heading: below: (cont.)
sections.title: Timelines for complaint resolution by financial institutions
sections.summary: This section specifies timeframes for resolving customer complaints across various financial services categories. It outlines the number of working days required for first and second reviews for each complaint type, including account management, cards, loans, fraud, and international transactions. The timelines apply to institutions regulated under the CBN Consumer Protection Regulations.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes timeframes for resolving customer complaints, which may impact data transfer timelines if complaint resolution involves cross-border data sharing or international transactions.
